text.title: Spring @Async Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree mapping observable signals to a rule from 01-core-rules.xml.

decision-tree.root-question: Does the work need to survive a JVM crash?

conclusion.verdict: skip-this-methodology — use Kafka / RabbitMQ / Spring Batch; in-memory executor loses work on crash
skip-this-methodology — use ForkJoinPool / Reactor; @Async pools are sized for IO

branch.question: Is the caller already inside @Transactional that should propagate?

conclusion.verdict: restructure — call @Async after commit via TransactionSynchronizationManager

branch.question: Does the async method use MDC / SecurityContext / tracing?

conclusion.verdict: install TaskDecorator before annotating any @Async method
apply remaining rules — named executor + CompletableFuture return + graceful shutdown

text.example: durability_required=false → work_type=io_bound → tx_propagation_required=false → context_required=true → install TaskDecorator + named auditExecutor + CompletableFuture<Void> return.
